root_text.sn56.36:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso yaṁ imasmiṁ jambudīpe tiṇakaṭṭhasākhāpalāsaṁ tacchetvā ekajjhaṁ saṁhareyya;
root_text.sn56.36:1.3: Sūlaṁ karitvā ye mahāsamudde mahantakā pāṇā te mahantakesu sūlesu āvuneyya, ye mahāsamudde majjhimakā pāṇā te majjhimakesu sūlesu āvuneyya, ye mahāsamudde sukhumakā pāṇā te sukhumakesu sūlesu āvuneyya.
root_text.sn56.36:1.4: Apariyādinnā ca, bhikkhave, mahāsamudde oḷārikā pāṇā assu.
root_text.sn56.36:2.1: Atha imasmiṁ jambudīpe tiṇakaṭṭhasākhāpalāsaṁ parikkhayaṁ pariyādānaṁ gaccheyya.
root_text.sn56.36:2.2: Ito bahutarā kho, bhikkhave, mahāsamudde sukhumakā pāṇā, ye na sukarā sūlesu āvunituṁ.
root_text.sn56.36:2.6: Evaṁ mahantasmā kho, bhikkhave, apāyasmā parimutto diṭṭhisampanno puggalo ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.sn56.36:3.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.36:1.1: “Suppose a person was to strip all the grass, sticks, branches, and leaves in the Black Plum Tree Land, gather them together into one pile,
translation_text.sn56.36:1.3: Then they’d impale the large creatures in the ocean on large stakes; the medium-sized creatures on medium-sized stakes; and the small creatures on small stakes.
translation_text.sn56.36:1.4: They wouldn’t run out of sizable creatures in the ocean before
translation_text.sn56.36:2.1: using up all the grass, sticks, branches, and leaves in the Black Plum Tree Land.
translation_text.sn56.36:2.2: There are far more small creatures in the ocean than this, so it wouldn’t be feasible to impale them on stakes.
translation_text.sn56.36:2.4: Because of the small size of those life-forms.
translation_text.sn56.36:2.5: That’s how big the plane of loss is.
translation_text.sn56.36:2.6: An individual accomplished in view, exempt from that vast plane of loss, truly understands: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.sn56.36:3.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
